title: caucalis latifolia var. tuberculata
intro: Caucalis latifolia var. tuberculata, also known as the Rough-fruited Cauliflower, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Caucalis latifolia var. tuberculata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves, stems, and roots in hot water or a solvent.
